name: Eric Gale
abstract: Eric Gale (September 20, 1938 – May 25, 1994) was an American jazz and jazz fusion guitarist.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: We did a lot of stuff that people wouldn’t associate with Benny Golson. ... With Eric Gale and Richard Tee – those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hank Cicalo
about_quotes.evidence: The A bands in New York for a long time was Richard Tee, Steve Gadd on drums, Ralph McDonald percussion, I mean Hugh McCracken on guitar, Eric Gale on guitar. Those were the A guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hank Cicalo interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 30, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: You talked about Eric Gale yesterday... You’re listed flute and piccolo on this one... Creed Taylor putting the rhythm section out there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: And guitar players was Mickey Baker, Eric Gale... I mean for me I could not have ever asked for anybody any better than those musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I want to work with R&B studio cats like Bernard Purdie and Richard Tee and Eric Gale and Chuck Rainey.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007
